Container.Name: AthenaScout
Container.Overview:
AthenaScout is a self-hosted book library completionist tracker. Point it at one or more Calibre libraries and it will cross-reference your collection against metadata sources like Goodreads, Hardcover, and Kobo to find books you're missing from authors and series you already collect.
Features include multi-library discovery, multi-source scanning, MyAnonamouse cross-referencing, import/export, series intelligence, Calibre-Web deep linking, single-admin authentication, and a responsive web UI with dark/dim/light themes. Built on a library-app framework so additional source applications (audiobookshelf, etc.) can be added in future releases.
Container.Description: Self-hosted book library completionist tracker. Discovers Calibre libraries automatically, finds missing books via Goodreads/Hardcover/Kobo, and cross-references against MyAnonamouse availability. Single-admin auth, multi-library, multi-theme, responsive UI.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Host port for the AthenaScout web interface

Config.Name: Calibre Library Root
Config.Description: Path to your Calibre library (or a parent folder containing multiple Calibre libraries). AthenaScout scans this path AND its immediate subdirectories for any folder containing a metadata.db, registering each as a separate library. Mounted read-only — AthenaScout never modifies your Calibre data.

Config.Name: App Data
Config.Description: Persistent storage for AthenaScout's databases (one SQLite file per discovered library), settings, auth credentials, and the auth signing secret. Survives container restarts and image updates.

Config.Name: CALIBRE_PATH
Config.Description: Primary discovery root for the Calibre library app. AthenaScout's library-app framework scans this path for libraries on startup. Leave at /calibre to use the volume mount above. Future ebook/audiobook source apps will use their own *_PATH variables.

Config.Name: CALIBRE_EXTRA_PATHS
Config.Description: Comma-separated list of additional library locations the Settings UI can browse. NOT auto-scanned at startup — use this when you want to manually add a library from a path other than the primary mount. Each path listed here must also have its own volume mount.

Config.Name: Calibre-Web URL
Config.Description: URL of your Calibre-Web instance (janeczku/calibre-web or calibre-web-automated). Enables deep 'Open in Calibre Web' links from individual books in the sidebar. Example: http://192.168.1.100:8083

Config.Name: Calibre Library URL
Config.Description: URL of your Calibre content server or main library UI. Shown as a quick-access button on the AthenaScout dashboard. Example: http://192.168.1.100:8181

Config.Name: Hardcover API Key
Config.Description: API key from hardcover.app (Account → API). Must include the 'Bearer ' prefix. Leave empty to configure via the Settings UI after first run.

Config.Name: MAM Session ID
Config.Description: MyAnonamouse mam_id session cookie for cross-referencing book availability. Optional — recommended to configure via the Settings UI instead so you can complete the IP-locking flow correctly.

Config.Name: MAM Scan Interval
Config.Description: Minutes between scheduled MAM scans of books missing MAM data (default 360 = 6 hours, 0 = disable scheduled scans, manual scans always work)

Config.Name: Calibre Sync Interval
Config.Description: Minutes between automatic Calibre library re-syncs (0 to disable scheduled syncs)

Config.Name: Source Scan Interval
Config.Description: Minutes between automatic metadata source scans (default 4320 = 3 days, 0 to disable scheduled scans, manual scans always work)

Config.Name: Auth Signing Secret
Config.Description: Optional: signing secret for session cookies (≥32 characters of high-entropy randomness). When set, AthenaScout uses this instead of writing a secret file to the data directory. Leave empty to use the default file-based fallback. See SECURITY.md for details.

Config.Name: Verbose Logging
Config.Description: Enable detailed debug logging on startup (true/false). Can also be toggled at runtime in the Settings UI.

Config.Name: WEBUI_PORT
Config.Description: Internal web UI port. Must match the container port in the Web UI Port mapping above.

Config.Name: CALIBRE_DB_PATH (legacy)
Config.Description: LEGACY single-library mode. Direct path to a single metadata.db inside the container. Leave empty to use the multi-library discovery via CALIBRE_PATH (recommended).

Config.Name: CALIBRE_LIBRARY_PATH (legacy)
Config.Description: LEGACY single-library mode. Path to the Calibre library root that owns the CALIBRE_DB_PATH file. Leave empty unless you set CALIBRE_DB_PATH above.
